title: Stock market today: Dow and S&P 500 climbs, Nasdaq bounces back as 'Magnificent 7' results lift AI hopes
body: Oops, something went wrong US stocks were choppy on Thursday as investors assessed a fresh batch of key economic data and a strong slate of earnings reports, particularly Big Tech results that fueled optimism about the AI demand boom. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite ( ^IXIC ) rose 0.2% after the index dipped in midmorning trading. The Dow Jones Industrial Average ( ^DJI ) climbed 1.1%, or more than 500 points, while the S&P 500 ( ^GSPC ) put on about 0.4% following a lackluster Wednesday for Wall Street stocks. In early morning trade, oil prices reached their highest level since 2022 on an Axios report that President Trump is looking at fresh military options against Iran. title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady as divisions emerge, Powell announces he'll stay on as governor
body: Oops, something went wrong The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady for the third consecutive policy meeting this year amid a surge in oil prices and increased economic uncertainty from the Iran war. The central bank voted in a split decision Wednesday to hold its benchmark interest rate in the range of 3.5% to 3.75%. Fed governor Stephen Miran disagreed, preferring to cut rates by a quarter percentage point. Cleveland Fed president Beth Hammack, Minneapolis Fed president Neel Kashkari, and Dallas Fed president Lorie Logan supported maintaining rates but disagreed with the “easing bias” implied in the policy statement and thus dissented. The last time there were four dissents was Oct. 6, 1992. The divisions emerged as Fed Chair Jerome Powell announced on Wednesday that he intends to

title: Oil surges over $100 per barrel as Trump says he will maintain Hormuz blockade, keeping pressure on global market
body: Oops, something went wrong Oil prices surged again on Wednesday after Trump said he would continue to enforce the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz in an attempt to bleed the Iranian economy. Futures on Brent crude ( BZ=F ), the international benchmark, gained roughly 6.5% to trade above $118 per barrel, according to Bloomberg data. Those on US benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude ( CL=F ) picked up up 6.4% to trade above $106 per barrel. Prices surged in midday trading after Trump told Axios he would be maintaining the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most critical waterway for global energy flows. Futures had begun to advance overnight after the Wall Street Journal reported that Trump told aides to prepare for a long-running blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gas prices jump to highest level since July 2022 as oil hovers near $110
body: Oops, something went wrong Gasoline prices jumped to their highest level since July 2022 on Wednesday as oil rose near $110 per barrel. The national average at the pump rose to nearly $4.23 per gallon, up about $0.12 over the past two days and more than $1 higher than a year ago, according to AAA data . Given the rapid climb in oil prices in recent days, analysts expect fuel costs to climb even higher as retail stations catch up with wholesale increases. “May could begin with prices in the $4.30's. If you prefer premium gas, good luck with average numbers over $5/gallon,” Tom Kloza, chief energy adviser at Gulf Oil, said in a note. title: Valero Energy Earnings Skyrocket As Key Metric Swells Amid Iran War
body: BREAKING: Fed's Inflation Gauge Hits Two-Year High, As Expected Investors.com is currently experiencing slowness. We are working to resolve the issue and apologize for any inconvenience. Valero Energy (VLO) easily beat earnings estimates for the first quarter early Thursday, after the Iran war caused refining margins to surge. Valero stock fell slightly after rallying into results. "We continue to be well-positioned to benefit from the current margin environment," Valero CEO Lane Riggs said in a statement.
